commit.message:
v2.4: Stacked filter effects + sharpie icon + App Store ready

Filter system overhaul:
- 80% of polas now get 1+ vintage effects with probability stacking
- 1% "mangled" treatment (angry ex crumpled/burned chaos)
- Multi-filter support in SavedPola model with backwards compatibility

Caption area enhancement:
- Sharpie icon indicator (80% height, right-aligned, -12° tilt)
- Caption text stays centered with balanced horizontal padding
- Canvas-based SVG rendering for crisp icon at any scale

App Store submission materials:
- APP_STORE_METADATA.md with subtitle, description, keywords
- APP_STORE_SUBMISSION.md step-by-step guide
- Version bumped to 2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add account system, auth, multi-tenancy, and freeform tags

- Family accounts with shared password and configurable per-user auth (none/PIN/password)
- Session-based auth with cookie, 11 API endpoints for registration, login, member selection, credential management
- Store key namespacing for multi-tenant data isolation ({familyId}:key)
- Auto-migration of existing data to default family with password reset prompt
- Freeform tags on users with tag-based task visibility filtering (requiredTags)
- New components: ps-auth-screen (login/register/member selection/PIN pad), ps-admin-security (auth level config, credential management)
- Setup wizard simplified to currency-only (admin creation moved to registration)
- Switch user now goes through auth member selection
- 401 handling in TrackerStore triggers re-auth flow
- Service worker cache bumped to v3 with new component files

commit.message:
feat(analytics): add daily_returns, monthly_returns, drawdown_series to RunResult

Add three series-returning methods for time-series analysis:
- daily_returns(): simple daily returns with date index
- monthly_returns(): month-end returns with period index
- drawdown_series(): daily drawdown from peak with date index

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: plan-first Kanban flow with approve, draft-plan, and epic-gated execution

- Add `plan` field to epics (schema + migration) for curated final plans
- Add `POST /approve` endpoint: validates plan, decomposes into tasks, sets epic to "ready"
- Add `POST /draft-plan` endpoint: AI merges chat sessions into a plan draft
- Add `GET /epics/:epicId/tasks` endpoint to list decomposed tasks per epic
- Engine only picks tasks from "implementing" epics (move to Implement = start execution)
- Remove standalone task creation (all tasks belong to epics)
- Decomposer now uses epic.plan as primary context when available
- Add `updateEpic()` for partial updates (title, description, plan, status)
- Add frontend.md with UI/UX design notes for the Kanban plan phase

Fix --all flag: include semibin, maxbin, lorbin, comebin

the --all shortcut in run-mag.sh, causing DAS Tool/Binette/MAGScoT to
only receive metabat + vamb + vamb_tax inputs.

commit.message:
Switch cover letters to Claude Sonnet + import ChatGPT conversations

1. Cover letter generation: swap OpenAI for Anthropic SDK (claude-sonnet-4-6).
   Better voice-matching and fewer generic AI patterns. Embeddings stay on
   OpenAI (text-embedding-3-small). ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + COVER_LETTER_MODEL
   set on Render.

2. POST /api/v1/import/import-chatgpt-conversations: upload conversations.json
   from a ChatGPT data export. Claude reads each conversation and extracts
   project stories → Memory records and cover letter examples → Proposal records
   (which already feed the cover letter prompt as voice calibration). Processes
   in batches of 5 conversations to stay within rate limits.

3. Memories page: adds a second import panel (green border) for conversations.json,
   distinct from the existing memory.json panel. Shows spinner + result summary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add user authentication, dashboard, DNS management and admin panel

Major features added:
- User registration and login system with secure password hashing
- Personal dashboard for managing owned domains
- DNS management with support for A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT records
- Admin panel with platform statistics
- Domain search and filtering functionality
- Enhanced API with user registration and stats endpoints
- Default admin account (admin/admin123)

Updated README with complete feature documentation and API usage examples.

or on manual trigger.

feat: replace dead buttons (Tidal Pulse, Random Effect) with Firework Show and Barrage

Telemetry shows Tidal Pulse had 0 uses and Random Effect had 3 uses across
165 sessions. Replaced them with two already-built spectacular effects that
were hidden behind keyboard-only shortcuts — Firework Show (5 sequential
firework bursts) and Barrage (12-orb volley from screen edge). Both effects
are well-aligned with user preferences for explosive visual spectacles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Emmet: split --browser into --functions (Playwright) and --personas (@playwright/mcp)

Two distinct testing modes with clear purposes:
- --functions: automated Playwright tests with principles P1-P9 (depth over breadth,
  exhaustive entity coverage, multi-step flows, API data integrity, mandatory report
  hooks, typed constants, graceful timeouts, bug regression, naming convention)
- --personas: Claude navigates via @playwright/mcp, embodies map personas, judges
  UX/UI/workflow with 1-5 scoring and qualitative report

fix: camera stream now reliably attaches to video element

- Replace fragile setTimeout(50ms) with useEffect watching cameraOpen
  so stream is attached only after React has mounted the <video> DOM node
- Add explicit video.play() call (required on some mobile browsers)
- Add onPlaying handler to track camera readiness (cameraReady state)
- Capture button disabled with "Waiting for camera..." until video plays
- Green/amber status dot in camera header shows ready state
- Guard capturePhoto: check videoWidth > 0 and readyState >= 2 before
  drawing to canvas — prevents silent empty-blob failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 429 rate limit causing stale usage data

When the Anthropic usage API returned 429, the file cache timestamp
was never updated, causing every subsequent call to retry the API
and get rate-limited again indefinitely. Now handles 429 with a
dedicated backoff (respects Retry-After header, defaults to 5min),
and updates cache timestamps on all error paths to prevent retry storms.

chore: remove 10 empty placeholder files superseded by Protocol design

These files were created as placeholders but are no longer needed:
- core/registry.py — Strategy composes components declaratively
- indicators/base.py, signals/base.py, rules/base.py — Protocol replaces ABC
- indicators/custom.py — custom indicators defined inline
- portfolio/types.py, portfolio/accounting.py, portfolio/portfolio.py —
  types live in core/types.py, accounting in engine._apply_fill()
- trade/types.py — Order/Fill live in core/types.py
- trade/generator.py — unclear purpose, no references

feat(TSK-15822): Enterprise plugin with Workflo sync (#85)

* feat(TSK-15822): add enterprise sync, Workflo API client, and Peakflo plugin enterprise mode

Adds enterprise integration between 20x desktop and Workflo:
- WorkfloApiClient: typed REST client for Workflo API (tasks, org nodes, skills)
- EnterpriseSyncManager: syncs agents, skills, MCP servers, task sources from Workflo org hierarchy to local SQLite
- PeakfloPlugin enterprise mode: fetches tasks via REST API instead of MCP when enterprise-connected
- PeakfloConfigForm: shows enterprise connection status when authenticated
- Auto-creates Peakflo task source on enterprise connect
- SyncManager wiring: runs enterprise sync before each task import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: probe OAuth on MCP server dialog open, not just on URL change

Previously, opening an existing remote MCP server that requires OAuth
would not show the OAuth section until the user removed and re-added the
URL. Now the dialog probes for auth automatically when opened with an
existing remote server URL.

commit.message:
Add git:ship skill for clean-commit-PR workflow

Encodes the workflow for shipping a feature across the multi-repo stack:
clean non-feature diffs (formatting, local dep overrides, scratch files),
review with Skyscanner production standards, create a feature branch,
stage files explicitly, commit with dependency callouts, push, and
open a PR with a dependency chain table and merge-order guidance.
